items.title: New NERC-funded project empowers young people to engage with environmental science - British Geological Survey
items.content_text: BGS is part of a new, collaborative project, funded through NERC’s ‘Opening up the environment’ programme, helping the environmental research community become more accessible.

items.title: Geo-Explorers - after-school kids' clubs - British Geological Survey
items.content_text: The primary aim of the meeting is networking within technology areas in NERC and related research centres.

items.title: Building Europe’s geological future - British Geological Survey
items.content_text: BGS scientists are contributing to a continent-wide initiative that is strengthening collaboration across borders to tackle shared subsurface challenges and unlock new opportunities.

items.title: Delving deep into mountains for future water security - British Geological Survey
items.content_text: A BGS-led project is using new water-tracing investigation methods, geophysics and modelling to understand how water moves from mountains to the lowlands, helping to improve water security for billions of people.

items.title: Collecting beach pebbles: fun hobby but is it legal? - British Geological Survey
items.content_text: The UK coastline will be inundated with keen holidaymakers this summer, many of whom will take a memento of their trip home in the shape of a pebble or two — but is it legal?

items.title: Member Webinar | Lessons from the Field: ADM's Experience Advancing Carbon
items.content_text: On December 15, 2025 the Institute hosted a Member Webinar highlighting key operational lessons learned from ADM’s CCS project in Decatur, Illinois.  The session explored the project’s current status and key operational learnings that inform large-scale carbon storage deployment in the United States and globally. The discussion highlighted lessons learned across project design, metallurgy, and […]

items.title: Updated national 3D model will determine the depth to the solid bedrock beneath our feet - British Geological Survey
items.content_text: The BGS Superficial Deposit Thickness Model now includes an elevation model of geological rockhead for the first time, providing important data for civil engineers, geoscientists and environmental scientists.

items.title: UK’s world-class research organisations launch new collective - British Geological Survey
items.content_text: The launch of the National Research Organisations Group, brings together more than 100 leaders from government, research, industry and funding organisations.

items.title: Cheshire Geoenergy Observatory releases key geological datasets for UK thermal energy storage scheme design - British Geological Survey
items.content_text: A new report and data packs from BGS will provide a unique resource for scientists working on the geology and hydrogeology of the Sherwood Sandstone Formation, an important aquifer and reservoir rock.

items.title: Webinar | Live from the UNFCCC Bonn Climate Change Conference
items.content_text: On 17 June 2026, the Institute hosted a live webinar from the UNFCCC Bonn Climate Change Conference, providing insights into the latest international climate negotiations and their implications for carbon capture and storage (CCS) and broader carbon management efforts on the road to COP31 in Antalya, Türkiye. Reporting directly from Bonn, the Institute’s Senior Global […]

items.title: 2026 New York Climate Week Side Event
items.content_text: Save the Date The Global CCS Institute is pleased to announce it will convene a select group of leaders during 2026 New York Climate Week for its annual, invitation-only side event, focused on CCS finance.   Date: Tuesday, September 22, 2026 Time: 12:00 PM – 7:00 PM We are grateful to once again be hosted by Latham & […]

items.content_text: About The Institute is delighted to invite you to join our Monthly Member Webinar series, where each month members and friends of the Institute gain insights from fellow members across a broad spectrum of topics in carbon capture and storage. items.title: CCS Capacity Building Workshop Poland
items.content_text: The Global CCS Institute is pleased to host the next Capacity Building Workshop in Poland. This session will bring together selected stakeholders to discuss the current status of CCS in Poland, explore successful business cases from regions where CCS projects are under development, and examine funding mechanisms and key challenges for local emitters. This workshop […]
